question: As shown in the figure. Determine the object’s velocity at times t = 8 s.
Determine the period of small oscillations of the assembly when \alpha = 0, as shown in the figure.
A disc can rotate about the axis XX’. The time period of oscillation of the disc is?
As shown in the figure What is the magnitude of the puck’s acceleration at t = 5 s?
The net force \( \vec{F} \) on the \( 1.0\,\mathrm{nC} \) charge in figure is zero. What is q?

A 10-m-high cylinder, with a cross-sectional area of 0.1 m^2, has a massless piston at the bottom with water at 20\circ\text{C} on top of it. Air at 300 K, with a volume of 0.3 m^3, under the piston is heated so that the piston moves up, spilling the water out over the side. Find the total heat transfer to the air when all the water has been pushed out.
Two point charges are placed on the \( x \)-axis as shown. Find the position on the \( x \)-axis where the net force on a small charge \( +q \) would be zero.

Suppose that a particle with mass \( m \) and energy \( E \) is moving toward the origin of a system \( S \) such that its velocity \( \mathbf{u} \) makes an angle \( \alpha \) with the \( y \) axis as shown in figure. determine the energy \( E' \) of the particle measured by an observer in \( S' \), which moves relative to \( S \) so that the particle moves along the \( y' \) axis.

In the laboratory, the measured velocity of an electron is as shown in figure. Suppose an observer moves in the same direction as the electron relative to the laboratory. Find the kinetic energy as measured by this observer. (The rest mass of the electron is $m_e = 9.11 \times 10^{-31} \text{ kg}$)

Two steady flows of air enter a control volume. One is a 0.025 kg/s flow at 350kPa, 150\circ\text{C}, state 1, and the other enters at 450 kPa, 15\circ\text{C}, state 2. A single flow exits at 100 kPa,−40\circ\text{C}, state 3. The control volume ejects 1 kW heat to the surroundings and produces 4 kW of power output. Neglect kinetic energies and determine the mass flow rate at state 2.

A charged sphere remains stationary while a particle of mass \(4.00 \times 10^{-4}\text{kg}\) and charge \(5.00 \times 10^{-8}\text{C}\) is fired toward it. The particle's speed \(v\) is measured as a function of distance \(x\), and the graph of \(v^2\) versus \(1/x\) fits \(v^2 = 400\text{m}^2/\text{s}^2 - (15.75\text{m}^3/\text{s}^2)/x\). What is Q?

Each charge shown is \(\pm e\), and the H--N distance is \(0.110\,\text{nm}\). Calculate the force on the electron in the hydrogen atom, which is \(0.0529\,\text{nm}\) from the proton. Then compare the strength of the bonding force of the electron in hydrogen with the bonding force of the adenine--thymine molecules.
Figure represents the total acceleration of a particle moving clockwise in a circle at a certain instant of time. For that instant, find its tangential acceleration.

In a set of experiments on a hypothetical one-electron atom, you measure the wavelengths of the photons emitted from transitions ending in the ground state \( n = 1 \), as shown in the energy-level diagram in the figure. You also observe that it takes \( 17.50 \, \text{eV} \) to ionize this atom. If an electron made a transition from the \( n = 4 \) to the \( n = 2 \) level, what wavelength of light would it emit?

Figure shows the deflecting plates of an ink-jet printer, with superimposed coordinate axes. An ink drop with a mass \( m = 1.3 \times 10^{-10}\ \mathrm{kg} \) and a negative charge of magnitude \( Q = 1.5 \times 10^{-13}\ \mathrm{C} \) enters the region between the plates, initially moving along the \( x \) axis with speed \( v_x = 18\ \mathrm{m/s} \). The length \( L \) of each plate is 1.6\ \mathrm{cm}. The plates are charged and thus produce an electric field at all points between them. A...
A valve in the cylinder shown in the figure has a cross-sectional area of 11 \cm^2\ with a pressure of 735 kPa inside the cylinder and 99 kPa outside. How large a force is needed to open the valve?
A gas undergoes a cyclic process. How much work is done per cycle by a gas following the $pV$ trajectory shown in the figure?

From the Earth's coordinate system, a spacecraft is launched from $x = 0$ and accelerates along the x-axis. At time $t$, the spacecraft's position is $x(t)$ as shown in the figure. Assuming an astronaut in the spacecraft constantly monitors the acceleration and finds the acceleration to be a constant $g$. When the spacecraft's velocity reaches $v$, what is the distance traveled, $x$?

Consider a potential well defined as \( U(x) = \infty \) for \( x < 0 \), \( U(x) = 0 \) for \( 0 < x < L \), and \( U(x) = U_0 > 0 \) for \( x > L \) as shown in the figure. Consider a particle with mass \( m \) and kinetic energy \( E < U_0 \) that is trapped in the well. The wave function must remain finite as \( x \to \infty \). What must the form of the function \( \psi(x) \) for \( x > L \) be in order to satisfy both the Schrödinger equation and this boundary condition at infinity?
A conducting sphere, as shown in the figure, with uniform magnetization is rotating about its $z$-axis with low speed. Find the potential difference between the equator and the pole.
As shown in the figure What is the wavelength?
Based on the figure Find the wavelength of the light.
Based on the figure What is the prism’s index of refraction?
The mirror in figure deflects a horizontal laser beam What is the angle \phi?
A 5.0 Hz wave is shown in the figure What is the wave speed?
Based on the figure Find the focal length of the contact lens that will permit the wearer to see clearly an object that is 25 cm in front of the eye.

The wave profiles at \( t = 0 \) and \( t = 1\,\mathrm{s} \) are shown as the solid and dashed lines in the figure, respectively. It is known that the particle at \( x = 0 \) travels a total distance of \( 4.5\,\mathrm{cm} \) during the time interval from \( 0 \) to \( 1\,\mathrm{s} \). What is the wave speed?
The sun subtends an angle \( \theta \) radius at the pole of a concave mirror of focal length \( f \). What is the diameter of image of sun formed by the mirror?

A Jurassic mosquito is discovered embedded in a chunk of amber, which has index of refraction 1.6. One surface of the amber is spherically convex with radius of curvature 3.0 mm. The mosquito's head happens to be on the central axis of that surface and, when viewed along the axis, appears to be buried 5.0 mm into the amber. How deep is it really?

The vibration graphs of the particles located at \(x_1 = 1\,\mathrm{m}\) and \(x_2 = 7\,\mathrm{m}\) are shown in the left and right figures, respectively. What is the wave speed, given that the speed is between 1 m/s and 2 m/s?

As shown in Figure, a thick lens with a refractive index of 1.5 has a radius of curvature of 3 cm for its lower surface. The central thickness of the lens is 2 cm, and it is placed on water. The refractive index of water is 4/3. An object with a height of 2 mm is placed below the thick lens on the optical axis in the water, and the distance between the object and the center of the lower surface of the thick lens is 4 cm. Find the height of the final image under the paraxial approximation.
Sunlight reflects off the smooth surface of a swimming pool. For what angle of reflection is the reflected light completely polarized?

A sinusoidal transverse wave with a period of \(2.0\,\mathrm{s}\) propagates along the \(x\)-axis. At a certain moment, the wave profile is as shown in the figure, and at this moment, particle \(P\) is moving in the negative direction of the \(y\)-axis. What is the speed of the wave?

Two sound sources S1 and S2 are separated, as shown in the figure. There are two observers at points A and B, which are on the same line as the sound sources. S1 emits a stable plane wave, whose excess pressure is given by $p_e = A \cos(200t)$; the excess pressure of the stable plane wave emitted by S2 is given by $p_e = A \cos(210t)$. The speed of sound is taken as 340 m s⁻¹. What is the time interval $\Delta t$ between the maximum pulses heard by A and B?

A circular converging lens, with diameter \( d = 32 \, \mathrm{mm} \) and focal length \( f = 24 \, \mathrm{cm} \), forms images of distant point objects in the focal plane of the lens. The wavelength is \( \lambda = 550 \, \mathrm{nm} \). What is the separation \( \Delta x \) of the centers of the image in the focal plane? (That is, what is the separation of the central peaks in the two intensity-versus-position curves?)

A \(5.00\,\text{m}\), \(0.732\,\text{kg}\) wire is used to support two uniform \(235\,\text{N}\) posts of equal length. Assume that the wire is essentially horizontal and that the speed of sound is \(344\,\text{m/s}\). A strong wind is blowing, causing the wire to vibrate in its 5th overtone. What is the wavelength of the sound this wire produces?
The thin lens shown in the figure has surface radii of 15.00 cm (left) and 10.00 cm (right). The refractive index of the glass is 1.5. Calculate the focal length of this lens.

A light ray of wavelength \(589\,\mathrm{nm}\) (produced by a sodium lamp) traveling through air is incident on a smooth, flat slab of crown glass at an angle \(\theta_1\) of \(30.0^\circ\) to the normal, as sketched in the figure. How does the answer for \(\theta_3\) change if the ray enters water below the slab instead of air?

A ray of light strikes the a–b interface at an angle that just barely exceeds its critical angle of 40.0\circ. The reflected ray then strikes the a–c interface at an angle of incidence that just barely exceeds its critical angle (which is not 40.0\circ). Find the indices of refraction in c material.

A parallel beam of light enters a glass hemisphere perpendicular to the flat face as shown in Figure P36.84. The magnitude of the radius of the hemisphere is \( R = 6.00 \text{ cm} \), and its index of refraction is \( n = 1.560 \). Assuming paraxial rays, determine the point at which the beam is focused.
Figure shows a Carnot heat engine driving a Carnot refrigerator. Determine $Q_3$.
0.020 mol of gas undergoes the process shown in the figure What is the final temperature?
0.0040 mol of gas undergoes the process shown in the figure What is the final temperature?
What is the thermal efficiency for the heat engine shown in the figure on a $p$-$V$ diagram?
A pipe flowing light oil has a manometer attached. What is the absolute pressure in the pipe flow?

A piston/cylinder assembly in a car contains $0.2 \, \text{L}$ of air at $90 \, \text{kPa}$ and $20 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$, as shown in the figure. The air is compressed in a quasi-equilibrium polytropic process with polytropic exponent $n = 1.25$ to a final volume six times smaller. Determine the heat transfer for the process.
Water at 1000 kPa, 250\circ\text{C} is brought to saturated vapor in a rigid container. Find the final temperature in this isometric process.

The figure shows the probability density for an electron that has passed through an experimental apparatus. What is the probability that the electron will land in a \(0.010\text{-mm}\)-wide strip at \(x = 0.000\text{ mm}\)?
The figure is a histogram showing the speeds of the molecules in a very small gas. Calculate and the rms speed v_{\text{rms}}.

Two kilograms of water at $120 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ with a quality of $25\%$ has its temperature raised $20 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ in a constant-volume process as in figure. What is the heat transfer in the process?

Liquid acetylene, C_2H_2, is stored in a high-pressure storage tank at ambient temperature, 25^\circ C. The liquid is fed to an insulated combustor/steam boiler at a steady rate of 1\ \mathrm{kg/s}, along with 140% theoretical oxygen, O_2, which enters at 500\ \mathrm{K}, as shown in figure. The combustion products exit the unit at 500\ \mathrm{kPa}, 350\ \mathrm{K}. Liquid water enters the boiler at 10^\circ C, at the rate of 15\ \mathrm{kg/s}, and superheated steam exits at 200\ \mathrm{kPa...

One kilogram per second water enters a solar collector at 40^\circ\mathrm{C} and exits at 190^\circ\mathrm{C}, as shown in figure. The hot water is sprayed into a direct-contact heat exchanger (no mixing of the two fluids) used to boil the liquid butane. Pure saturated-vapor butane exits at the top at 80^\circ\mathrm{C} and is fed to the turbine. If the butane condenser temperature is 30^\circ\mathrm{C} and the turbine and pump isentropic efficiencies are each 80\%, determine the net power ou...

A mixing chamber with heat transfer receives 2 kg/s of R-410a, at 1 MPa, 40\circ\text{C} in one line and 1 kg/s of R-410a at 15\circ\text{C} with a quality of 50% in a line with a valve. The outgoing flow is at 1 MPa, 60\circ\text{C}. Find the rate of heat transfer to the mixing chamber.

A rigid tank is divided into two rooms, both containing water, by a membrane. Room A is at 200 kPa, v = 0.5 m^3/kg, VA = 1 m^3, and room B contains 3.5 kg at 0.5 MPa, 400\circ\text{C}. The membrane now ruptures and heat transfer takes place so that the water comes to a uniform state at 100\circ\text{C}. Find the heat transfer.

To refresh air in a room, a counterflow heat exchanger is mounted in the wall, drawing in outside air at \(0.5^\circ \mathrm{C},\ 80\%\) relative humidity and pushing out room air at \(40^\circ \mathrm{C},\ 50\%\) relative humidity. Assume an exchange of \(3\,\mathrm{kg/min}\) dry air in a steady-flow device, and also assume that the room air exits the heat exchanger at \(23^\circ \mathrm{C}\) to the outside. Find the \(\phi\) for the fresh air entering the room.

A U-tube manometer filled with water (density = 1000 kg/m$^3$) shows a height difference of 25 cm. What is the gauge pressure? If the right branch is tilted to make an angle of $30^{\circ}$ with the horizontal, as shown in figure, what should the length of the column in the tilted tube be relative to the U-tube?

The piston/cylinder contains 0.1 kg water at 500\circ\text{C}, 1000 kPa. The piston has a stop at half of the original volume. The water now cools to a room temperature of 25\circ\text{C}. What is the heat transfer in the process?

You are conducting experiments to study prototype heat engines. In one test, 4.00 mol of argon gas are taken around the cycle shown in figure. The pressure is low enough for the gas to be treated as ideal. You measure the gas temperature in states $a$, $b$, $c$, and $d$ and find $T_a = 250.0$ K, $T_b = 300.0$ K, $T_c = 380.0$ K, and $T_d = 316.7$ K. Calculate the efficiency $e$ of the cycle.

A vertical cylinder fitted with a piston contains $5\,\text{kg}$ of R-410a at $10\,^{\circ}\text{C}$, as shown in the figure. Heat is transferred to the system, causing the piston to rise until it reaches a set of stops, at which point the volume has doubled. Additional heat is transferred until the temperature inside reaches $50\,^{\circ}\text{C}$, at which point the pressure inside the cylinder is $1.4\,\text{MPa}$. Calculate the heat transfer for the overall process.

A 1-m^3 rigid tank contains 100 kg R-410a at a temperature of 15\circ\text{C}. A valve on top of the tank is opened, and saturated vapor is throttled to ambient pressure, 100 kPa and flows to a collector system. During the process, the temperature inside the tank remains at 15\circ\text{C} by heat transfer from the 20\circ\text{C} ambient. The valve is closed when no more liquid remains inside. Calculate the total entropy generation in the process.
Based on the figure. Determine the capacitive reactance of the capacitor.
The electric potential at the dot in figure is 3140 V. What is charge q?
Analyze a circuit with several capacitors in parallel. Find the total charge contained in the configuration.
Find the current \( I_1 \) in the circuit shown in Figure 18.14 by using Kirchhoff's rules.
The figure shows two uniformly charged spheres. How much higher is b's potential compared to a?

A coil with 4 turns, radius \(0.800\ \text{cm}\), and resistance \(0.250\ \Omega\) is placed between the poles of an electromagnet whose field increases from zero at \(t = 0\); the current \(i\) induced in the coil as a function of time \(t\) is shown. Calculate the magnetic field at the location of the coil for \(t = 6.00\ \text{s}\).

A coaxial cable consists of an insulated wire carrying current \( I_1 = 3.00 \, \mathrm{A} \) surrounded by a cylindrical conductor carrying current \( I_2 = 1.00 \, \mathrm{A} \) in the opposite direction, as in figure. Calculate the magnetic field outside the cylindrical conductor at \( r_{\mathrm{ext}} = 1.50 \, \mathrm{cm} \).
Charge \( Q \) is uniformly distributed around a conducting ring of radius \( a \). Find the electric field at a point \( P \) on the ring axis at a distance \( x \) from its center.
Figure shows a 10-cm-diameter loop in magnetic fields. The loop's resistance is \(0.20\,\Omega\). What is the size of the induced current?

Two long, coaxial cylindrical conductors are separated by vacuum. The inner cylinder has outer radius \( r_a \) and linear charge density \( +\lambda \). The outer cylinder has inner radius \( r_b \) and linear charge density \( -\lambda \). Find the capacitance per unit length for this capacitor.
In the circuit, the switch \( S \) has been closed for a long time. What length of time passes between subsequent \( 10.0\,\text{V} \) output voltages?

A radio station on the earth’s surface emits a sinusoidal wave with average total power 50\ \mathrm{kW} Assuming that the transmitter radiates equally in all directions above the ground (which is unlikely in real situations) find the electric-field and magnetic-field amplitudes \( E_\mathrm{max} \) and \( B_\mathrm{max} \) detected by a satellite 100\ \mathrm{km} from the antenna.

A Geiger counter operates by ionizing air and detecting current pulses. The central wire has radius \(145\mu\text{m}\), and the surrounding cylinder has radius \(1.80\text{cm}\). What potential difference between the wire and the cylinder produces an electric field of \(2.00 \times 10^4\text{V/m}\) at a distance of \(1.20\text{cm}\) from the axis of the wire?

Suppose the long solenoid in figure has 500 turns per meter and cross-sectional area \( 4.0 \\ \mathrm{cm}^2 \). The current in its windings is increasing at \( 100 \\ \mathrm{A/s} \). Find the magnitude of the induced electric field within the loop if its radius is \( 2.0 \\ \mathrm{cm} \).
Figure shows a thin rod of length L with total charge Q. Evaluate \( E \) at \( r = 3.0\,\mathrm{cm} \) if \( L = 5.0\,\mathrm{cm} \) and \( Q = 3.0\,\mathrm{nC} \).
In the circuit shown in Figure, the driving frequency is $\omega = 1/\sqrt{LC}$. Find the maximum current $I_{L, \text{max}}$ through $L$.

In the circuit, the switch \( S \) has been open for a long time and is suddenly closed. The battery and inductors have negligible resistance. What does the voltmeter read 0.115 ms after \( S \) is closed?

In the circuit, a \(20.0\,\Omega\) resistor is inside 100 g of pure water that is surrounded by insulating Styrofoam. If the water is initially at \(10.0^\circ\text{C}\), how long will it take for its temperature to rise to \(58.0^\circ\text{C}\)?

Your camping buddy has an idea for a light to go inside your tent. He happens to have a powerful (and heavy!) horseshoe magnet that he bought at a surplus store. This magnet creates a field between two pole tips \(10\,\text{cm}\) apart. His idea is to build the hand-cranked generator shown in the figure. He thinks you can make enough current to fully light a lightbulb. That's not super bright, but it should be plenty of light for routine activities in the tent. With what frequency will you ha...

The magnetic field between the poles of the electromagnet in figure is uniform at any time, but its magnitude is increasing at the rate of $0.020 \, \text{T/s}$. Find the induced current in the circuit.
As shown in the figure. Determine the amount of tension in the rope.
What is \( V_\text{C} \) if the emf frequency in figure is \( 10\,\text{kHz} \)?
Under the double-slit condition shown in the figure, find where the first minimum appears.
Based on the figure How long is the streak of reflected light across the floor?
The emf phasor is shown in the figure. What is the instantaneous value of the emf?
As shown in the figure What is the horizontal distance d between the conveyer belt and the pipe?

A train is traveling through a (rest) length $L_0$ tunnel as shown in gigure. Find the time experienced by the passenger from the time the front of the train enters the tunnel to the time the tail of the train exits the tunnel.

A firefighter, a distance from a burning building, directs a stream of water from a fire hose as shown in figure. If the initial speed of the stream is \( v_i \), at what height \( h \) does the water strike the building?

A retroreflector is an optical device that can reflect an incident light ray back along its original path. A retroreflector of the Sugden sphere type is made by silvering the rear hemisphere of a transparent spherical medium to create a reflective surface. By selecting an appropriate refractive index $n$, the incident light ray can be reflected back along its original path as shown in the figure. Determine the refractive index of the material of the sphere.

Suppose a spaceship is launched from Earth, travels in a straight line to the Moon as shown in the figure. The speed of the spaceship is measured on Earth. How long does the trip take according to the measurements on the spaceship?

In figure, Sally (at point \( A \)) and Sam's spaceship (of proper length \( L_0 = 230 \, \mathrm{m} \)) pass each other with constant relative speed \( v \). Sally measures a time interval of \( 3.57 \, \mu\mathrm{s} \) for the ship to pass her. In terms of \( c \), what is the relative speed \( v \) between Sally and the ship?

An observer \( O \) is standing on a platform on a space station. A rocket passes at a relative moving parallel to the edge of the platform. The observer \( O \) notes that the front and back of the rocket simultaneously line up with the ends of the platform at a particular instant as shown in figure. According to \( O' \), how long does it take for observer \( O \) to pass the entire length of the rocket?

A steel tank of cross-sectional area $3\,\text{m}^2$ and height $16\,\text{m}$ weighs $10{,}000\,\text{kg}$ and is open at the top, as shown in the figure. We want to float it in the ocean so that it is positioned $10\,\text{m}$ straight down by pouring concrete into its bottom. How much concrete should we use?

Vector \( \vec{A} \) has magnitude 6 units and is in the direction of the \( +x \)-axis. Vector \( \vec{B} \) has magnitude 4 units and lies in the xy-plane. Find the vector product \( \vec{C} = \vec{A} \times \vec{B} \).

A rectangular parallelepiped as shown in figure. Notice that \( \vec{R}_1 \), \( c\hat{k} \), and \( \vec{R}_2 \) make a right triangle. Obtain a vector expression for the body diagonal vector \( \vec{R}_2 \).
A ray of light falls on a transparent sphere with centre at \( C \) as shown in the figure. The ray emerges from the sphere parallel to the line \( AB \). What is the refractive index of the sphere?
The conditions are as shown in the figure. Assume that the properties of salt water are the same as those of pure water, and that the pump is reversible and adiabatic. Determine q_H.
A motorcyclist heading east through a small town accelerates after he leaves the city limits as shown in figure. Where is he when his speed is \(25\,\text{m/s}\)?

In the figure, three spaceships are in a chase. Relative to an \(x\) axis in an inertial frame (say, Earth frame), their velocities are \(v_A = 0.900c\), \(v_B\), and \(v_C = 0.800c\). What value of \(v_B\) is required such that ships \(A\) and \(C\) approach ship \(B\) with the same speed relative to ship \(B\)?

A thief hides a precious jewel by placing it on the bottom of a public swimming pool. He places a circular raft on the surface of the water directly above and centered over the jewel, as shown in the figure. The surface of the water is calm. The raft, of diameter \(d = 4.54\ \text{m}\), prevents the jewel from being seen by any observer above the water, either on the raft or on the side of the pool. What is the maximum depth \(h\) of the pool for the jewel to remain unseen?
Figure shows a frustum of a cone. Calculate the area of the curved surface.
The graph in figure was measured in a photoelectric-effect experiment. What experimental value of Planck’s constant is obtained from these data?
The curve shown in the figure was obtained in a photoelectric effect experiment. Determine Planck's constant \(h\) from the data in the figure.
The figure is a graph of \( E_{x} \). What is the potential difference between \( x_{i} = 1.0 \ \mathrm{m} \) and \( x_{f} = 3.0 \ \mathrm{m} \)?
The figure is a graph of \( E_{x} \). The potential at the origin is \( -50 \ \mathrm{V} \). What is the potential at \( x = 3.0 \ \mathrm{m} \)?
The vibration curve of a vibrating particle is shown in the figure. Find the time required to reach the position corresponding to point P from the start of the vibration.
The figure shows a graph of \( V \) versus \( x \) in a region of space. The potential is independent of \( y \) and \( z \). What is \( E_{x} \) at \( x = 2\ \mathrm{cm} \)?

One cosmic-ray particle approaches Earth along Earth's north-south axis with a speed toward the geographic north pole, and another approaches with a speed toward the geographic south pole. What is the relative speed of approach of one particle with respect to the other?

Figure is a hypothetical plot of the recessional speeds \( v \) of galaxies against their distance \( r \) from us; the best-fit straight line through the data points is shown. From this plot determine the age of the universe, assuming that Hubble's law holds and that Hubble's constant has always had the same value.

A highway curve forms a section of a circle. A car goes around the curve as shown in the helicopter view of figure. Its dashboard compass shows that the car is initially heading due east. After it travels \( d = 840 \text{ m} \), it is heading \( \theta = 35.0^\circ \) south of east. Find the radius of curvature of its path.

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: A hypothetical atom is shown in figure. What is the highest frequencies of the spectral lines this atom can emit when excited?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: As shown in the figure. The center electron is displaced horizontally by a relatively small distance, what will its speed be when it is very far away?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: An electron at point \( A \) has a speed \( v_0 = 1.41 \times 10^6\,\text{m/s} \). Find the time required for the electron to move from A to B.

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Figure shows a pulse train. What is the maximum pulse-transmission rate (pulses per second) through an electronics system with a \(200\ \text{kHz}\) bandwidth? (This is the bandwidth allotted to each FM radio station.)

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: A photon scatters off a free electron at A as shown in the figure, producing a second photon. This photon then scatters off another free electron at B, producing a third photon. Determine the value of \(\Delta \lambda\).

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Figure shows the decay of parents in a radioactive sample. The axes are scaled by $N_s = 2.00 \times 10^6$ and $t_s = 10.0 \, \text{s}$. What is the activity of the sample at $t = 27.0 \, \text{s}$?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Nuclei of a radioactive element $A$ are being produced at a constant rate $\alpha$. The element has a decay constant $\lambda$. At time $t = 0$, there are $N_0$ nuclei of the element as shown in figure. Calculate the number $N$ of nuclei of $A$ at time $t$

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: A radionuclide with half-life $T = 14.3\ \text{days}$ is produced in a reactor at a constant rate $q = 10^9$ nuclei per second, as shown in the figure. How soon after the beginning of production of that radionuclide will its activity be equal to $A = 10^8$ disintegrations per second?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Characteristic X-rays of frequency $4.2 \times 10^{18} \text{ Hz}$ are produced when transitions from $L$ shell to $K$ shell take place in a certain target material. Use Moseley's law to determine the atomic number of the target material. Given Rydberg constant $R = 1.1 \times 10^{7} \text{ m}^{-1}$.

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Figure shows dust particles (\( m = 1.0 \times 10^{-15} \, \text{kg} \)) in a vacuum chamber. The dust particles are released from rest above a hole, fall through the hole (there's just barely room for the particles to go through), and land on a detector below. Quantum effects would be noticeable if the detection-circle diameter increased by \(10\%\) to \(1.1 \, \mu\text{m}\). At what distance \(d\) would the detector need to be placed to observe this increase in the diameter?
In one form of Tesla coil. Find the mutual inductance \( M \).
For the circuit shown in the figure, what is the resonance frequency, in \( \text{Hz} \)?
Analyze the RL circuit shown in the figure. What is \( V_\text{R} \) in the limit \( \omega \to 0 \)?
In the circuit, both batteries have insignificant internal resistance and the idealized ammeter reads \(1.50\,\text{A}\) in the direction shown. Find the emf \(\mathcal{E}\) of the battery.
